description_long: This is a medium-sized, round terracotta bowl with a reddish-brown hue and a slightly flared rim. The bowl features a decorative band with repeating geometric and possibly floral motifs encircling its exterior just below the rim. Several visible cracks and chips are present along the rim, indicating age or wear, and some areas appear to have been repaired. The interior is smooth and unadorned, while the exterior surface is matte with a slightly rough texture. The bowl is hollow and open, suitable for holding or serving items. Its overall appearance suggests it is hand-crafted, possibly for utilitarian or decorative purposes.
description: A reddish-brown terracotta bowl with decorative exterior motifs, visible cracks, and a matte, slightly rough finish.
description_view_0: This view shows the bowl's rim with two prominent chips and a clear view of the decorative band on the exterior.
description_view_1: This angle highlights a repaired section on the rim and the continuous decorative pattern, with fewer visible chips.
description_view_2: This side displays the largest area of rim damage and repair, with the decorative motifs still visible below.
description_view_3: This view shows a relatively intact rim section and a clear look at the bowl's smooth interior.
attribute_decorative_patterns: A band of repeating geometric and possibly floral motifs encircling the exterior just below the rim.
